name: Legal Practice
description: Intake, conflict checks and engagement letters, with a Matter object that carries the practice area, fee and court date after the retainer clears.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a law firm. Contacts are clients, opposing parties, witnesses, experts and referral sources; a deal is a prospective engagement that closes when the client signs the engagement letter and the retainer funds clear, after which the work itself is tracked as a Matter. Never give legal advice, never cite statutes or case law, and never state or calculate a limitation period, filing deadline or court date that is not already recorded on the record. If someone asks a substantive legal question, say it needs the responsible lawyer.

automation_rules.name: Conflict check before anything else
automation_rules.description: Every new contact gets a conflict check task before the firm gives any advice or takes any documents.

params.title: Run a conflict check on {{contact.first_name}} {{contact.last_name}} before advising
New enquiry received
params.body: Record how they found us, the other parties involved, and what they think they need. Do not open a matter file until the conflict check is cleared.

automation_rules.name: File note on every stage move
automation_rules.description: Fires whenever a deal changes stage, so the matter file always has a written record of where the engagement stands.

params.title: Update the file note and fee estimate for {{deal.title}}

kb_templates.company:
## About the firm

[Firm name] is a [size]-lawyer practice in [city], founded in [year]. We act mainly for [client type — e.g. owner-managed businesses, private individuals, insurers].

## Practice areas

[List the areas you actually take on, and the ones you refer out.]

## Contact

Office address, switchboard, out-of-hours number, and the name of the office manager.
kb_templates.products:
## How we charge

- **Hourly** — [rate band by seniority, e.g. partner / associate / paralegal].
- **Fixed fee** — [which matter types you will quote flat, e.g. conveyancing, company formation, simple wills].
- **Monthly retainer** — [what the retainer buys and what falls outside it].
- **Contingency / conditional** — [only where permitted in your jurisdiction; state your uplift].

Disbursements (court fees, searches, experts, counsel) are billed at cost and estimated in the engagement letter.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Intake call

1. Who are all the parties, including the other side? You cannot clear conflicts without names.
2. What actually happened, in date order, and what documents exist.
3. Is anything already listed — a hearing, a filing, a contractual notice period?
4. What outcome does the client want, and what would they settle for?
5. Fee expectation and who is paying.

## After the consultation

Send the scope and fee proposal within two working days. State clearly what is in scope, what is not, and what you need from the client to start.
kb_templates.process:
## Enquiry to open matter

1. **New Enquiry** — log the contact, the other parties and how they found us.
2. **Conflict Check** — run every name through the conflicts database. Nothing substantive happens until this clears.
3. **Consultation Held** — first meeting done, facts and documents captured.
4. **Scope & Fee Proposed** — written scope, fee basis and disbursement estimate sent.
5. **Engagement Letter Out** — letter and terms of business issued for signature.
6. **Retainer Received** — signed letter back and funds cleared into client account. Open the Matter record here.
7. **Lost** — no instruction, conflicted out, or referred elsewhere. Record which.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Where we win

Clients choose us over [larger regional firm] and [low-cost online provider] because [your genuine reason — a named specialist, sector experience, response times, fixed-fee certainty].

## Where we lose

We usually lose on [price / panel appointments / capacity for very large matters]. When that happens, [say what you do — refer out, offer a scoped-down fixed fee, keep the contact warm].
